mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor market conditions force wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding signal a synchronized contraction in household balance sheets, which reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or single-series models outperform the interaction term in out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply chain bottlenecks ease, lowering input costs and expanding corporate margins, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns do not significantly exceed baseline when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal flips direction during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rates signals expanding economic activity, which improves corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of consecutive positive GDP print instances, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation with SPX returns remains below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy tightens financial conditions, raising borrowing costs for equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment levels combined with stagnant industrial production indicate a structural shift toward services, which reduces capital expenditure intensity and compresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy over a baseline model using PAYEMS alone, or forward 60-day returns are not significantly lower when the divergence widens.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising consumer prices erode real disposable income, suppressing retail demand and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not decline significantly when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's predictive power disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS adjustments.
